IsA: It is a painting made of egg tempera and gold leaf on a wood panel.
It is a rare depiction of the Virgin and Child with Moses.
It is a visual story of Moses's encounters with the divine on Mount Sinai.
It is a painting in the traditional Cretan style.
HasA: It has figures of Moses and John of Damascus in the foreground.
It has a depiction of the Virgin and Child enthroned within the Burning Bush.
It has a scene of Moses striking the rock with his holy staff.
It has a depiction of the Golden calf.
It has God and angels passing the tablets to Moses.
It has angels bringing Saint Catherine's body to Jebel Katrina.
It has three mountains forming a triangle.
It has receding space for the Burning Bush.
It has drapery folds promoting three-dimensionality.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the use of egg tempera and gold leaf on a wood panel.
It is characterized by its rare depiction of the Virgin and Child with Moses.
It is characterized by the Italian renaissance cangiante technique.
It is characterized by the traditional hodegetria stance of the Virgin.
It is characterized by receding space for the Burning Bush.
It is characterized by three-dimensionality in the drapery folds.
It is characterized by three mountains forming a triangle.
It is characterized by traditional Cretan style painting.
It is characterized by a visual distinction of spatial depth.
UsedFor: It is used for creating a visual story of Moses's encounters with the divine on Mount Sinai.
It is used for depicting the Virgin and Child with Moses.
It is used for serving as an inspiration to Ieremias Palladas's painting of Saint Catherine.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Monastery of Agia Aikaterini in Heraklion, Crete.
BuiltIn: It was built in the second half of the 16th century.
CreatedBy: It was created by Greek painter Michael Damaskinos.
ConsistsOf: It consists of egg tempera and gold leaf on a wood panel.
It consists of a depiction of the Virgin and Child with Moses.
It consists of scenes from Moses's life from the Book of Exodus.
It consists of John of Damascus holding a scroll.
It consists of God and angels passing tablets to Moses.
It consists of angels bringing Saint Catherine's body to Jebel Katrina.
It consists of three mountains forming a triangle.
It consists of figures in the foreground of Moses and John of Damascus.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Greek painter Michael Damaskinos.
It is associated with the Cretan school of painting.
It is associated with Moses.
It is associated with the Burning Bush narrative.
It is associated with John of Damascus.
It is associated with the Monastery of Agia Aikaterini.
It is associated with the Book of Exodus.
It is associated with Catherine of Alexandria.
It is associated with the Italian renaissance cangiante.
It is associated with the hodegetria stance.
About: It is about the Virgin and Child with Moses.
It is about Moses's encounters with the divine on Mount Sinai.
It is about biblical narratives from the Book of Exodus.
It is about the Burning Bush narrative.
It is about John of Damascus's writings on the Burning Bush.
It is about the Virgin enthroned in the Burning Bush.
It is about the traditional hodegetria stance.
It is about the spatial depth in painting.
It is about the Cretan style of painting.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Italian artistic mannerisms adopted by Damaskinos.
It was influenced by the biblical narrative of Moses and the Burning Bush.
It was influenced by the writings of Byzantine theologian John of Damascus.
It was influenced by the traditional hodegetria stance.
It was influenced by the Cretan style of painting.
It was influenced by Damaskinos's technique.
It was influenced by the Adoration of the Kings painting.
Influenced: It influenced Ieremias Palladas's painting of Saint Catherine.
